author: • 3rd+
post_text: Follow-up to my Reopen Rate post — and a peek at one of my favorite QA metrics. After I shared the story of our 50% reopen rate and why I felt QA could’ve done more to prevent it, I got a lot of feedback (Thank you all for it!) Some agreed, some - did not. Some thought I was too harsh. So here’s my honest follow-up — and something practical that might help. 🙈 𝗬𝗲𝘀, 𝗜 𝘄𝗮𝘀 𝗯𝗹𝗮𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗤𝗔. But only a little — and only because I believe in its potential and the value it brings! There were real challenges on the development side: manual deployments, broken release processes, missing quality gates, and a disconnect with business. The PM was doing their best, pulling together whatever metrics he thought might help. But QA could’ve stepped in with insights. Highlighted patterns. Helped the team see what was really going on. Instead, they stayed stuck in routine — and most importantly, 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘆 𝗱𝗶𝗱𝗻’𝘁 𝗸𝗻𝗼𝘄 𝘄𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝘁𝗼 𝘁𝗿𝗮𝗰𝗸. That’s where the opportunity was lost. QA isn’t about ticking boxes — it’s about seeing the system. And speaking of systems… 📉 One of my favorite views: 𝗛𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗶𝗰𝗮𝗹 𝗗𝗮𝗶𝗹𝘆 𝗧𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗱 𝗼𝗳 𝗛𝗶𝗴𝗵 𝗣𝗿𝗶𝗼𝗿𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗗𝗲𝗳𝗲𝗰𝘁𝘀. And It’s not just a metric — it’s like a project’s photo album. You can literally 𝘴𝘦𝘦 the mood swings over time. Here’s mine : 🐰 Meet the Magic Bunny — the quiet Analyst who always knows what happened at those yellow arrows... Not tracking your own “defect family album”? 😆 Missing those memorable spikes and drops? No problem — here’s a simple JIRA query to look back in time: 𝘪𝘴𝘴𝘶𝘦𝘵𝘺𝘱𝘦 = 𝘉𝘶𝘨 𝘈𝘕𝘋 𝘴𝘵𝘢𝘵𝘶𝘴 𝙬𝙖𝙨 𝘪𝘯 ("𝘛𝘰 𝘋𝘰", "𝘐𝘯 𝘗𝘳𝘰𝘨𝘳𝘦𝘴𝘴") 𝘖𝘕 "2024-12-01" Extend it in Excel, and you’ve got a timeline. 🧩 Quick tip: use TextJoin function to generate multiple JIRA queries — one per day (month) — so you can go back and recreate your project’s defect story day by day. Something like: =𝘛𝘌𝘟𝘛𝘑𝘖𝘐𝘕(,𝘍𝘈𝘓𝘚𝘌,"𝘪𝘴𝘴𝘶𝘦 𝘵𝘺𝘱𝘦=𝘣𝘶𝘨 𝘢𝘯𝘥 𝘴𝘵𝘢𝘵𝘶𝘴 𝘸𝘢𝘴 𝘪𝘯 (""𝘛𝘰 𝘋𝘰"", ""𝘐𝘯 𝘱𝘳𝘰𝘨𝘳𝘦𝘴𝘴""", ")", " 𝘰𝘯 ", 𝘛𝘌𝘟𝘛(A2, "𝘺𝘺𝘺𝘺-𝘮𝘮-𝘥𝘥")) Easy, fast, and bunny-approved. 🐰 Build it? Great. Remember what caused every spike and dip? No?? Ask the bunny. 🐰 Let’s trade insights — and maybe teach the bunny a few things too! Metrics matter. Patterns speak. And sometimes… 🐰 the bunny sees it first. hashtag #EngineeringManagement hashtag #QualityEngineering hashtag #AgileTesting hashtag #DefectTrends hashtag #AskTheBunny hashtag #MagicBunnyKnows hashtag #DataDrivenQA

author: Natalia Moyseyenko
post_text: 🚨 Reopen Rate: The Silent Killer of Efficiency in Software Delivery — Or How Unprofessional QA Can Sink a Project and a Customer Budget I was once asked to audit a project that had been supported by a third-party QA vendor for 1.5 years. The defect backlog? 300+ issues. Not ideal — but not immediately alarming. Until I noticed this: 🔹 Reopen Rate = nearly 50% 🔹 Some defects were reopened up to 8 times! 🔹 Critical bugs averaged ~3.4 reopens each Let’s do the math: 🔸 min 1 hour per reopen cycle (Log ➝ Analyze ➝ Fix ➝ Deploy ➝ Retest ➝ Return) 🔸 8 reopens = a full day of wasted effort per issue (for 2 people min) 🔸 150+ issues = hundreds of hours and $$$ lost The real issue? Lack of visibility (from QA!). ▪️ QA never reported Reopen Rate ▪️ No trend analysis, no alerts, no cost tracking ▪️ 𝗝𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝗲𝘅𝗲𝗰𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 — 𝗻𝗼 𝗶𝗻𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 📊 This is exactly where Quality Engineering makes a difference. Selecting the right metric is an art — and it matters. I’ve previously shared 5 essential defect metrics, with Reopen Rate as one of them. Now you can see why. And yes — I have 50+ more I’d recommend, depending on your team’s context, goals, and maturity. 📌 Want to track it? Start here: JIRA Query: 𝘴𝘵𝘢𝘵𝘶𝘴 𝘊𝘏𝘈𝘕𝘎𝘌𝘋 𝘍𝘙𝘖𝘔 ("𝘋𝘰𝘯𝘦", "𝘊𝘭𝘰𝘴𝘦𝘥", "𝘙𝘦𝘴𝘰𝘭𝘷𝘦𝘥") 𝘛𝘖 ("𝘐𝘯 𝘗𝘳𝘰𝘨𝘳𝘦𝘴𝘴", "𝘛𝘰 𝘋𝘰", "𝘖𝘱𝘦𝘯") To calculate how many times each issue was reopened, use: 🐍 Python (JIRA API + Changelog Analysis) Use Python to extract issue histories and count how often issues were reactivated — either manually or with help from AI-driven scripts (or feel free to use the example from my screenshot!). 📊 Power BI (group by issue key or fix versions) 💬 Drop a +1 in the comments if you'd like me to help with QA metrics or Power BI/Python setup. hashtag #QualityEngineering hashtag #QAMetrics hashtag #ReopenRate hashtag #SoftwareTesting hashtag #TestManagement hashtag #StrategicQA hashtag #PowerBI hashtag #PythonForQA hashtag #JiraTips hashtag #CostOfQuality hashtag #QELeadership

author: Mohammad Faisal Khatri
post_text: You've just released a product but QA finds a critical issue. How do you navigate this post-release crisis? Checkout this blog where I discuss points of actions to be taken by a QA on finding a Critical Issue in Production https://lnkd.in/dgAq4w-B hashtag #QA hashtag #Release hashtag #SoftwareTesting hashtag #Production hashtag #Bugs hashtag #Testing
